title: sambucus nigra f. albomarmorata
intro: Sambucus nigra f. albomarmorata, also known as the white-flowered elder, is a variant of the common elder tree (Sambucus nigra) characterized by its white-marbled leaves and berries. This plant is traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiviral, and antimicrobial properties, due to the presence of flavonoids, alkaloids, and anthocyanins. The health benefits of Sambucus nigra f. albomarmorata include relieving symptoms of colds and flu, reducing inflammation, and supporting immune function. Herbal preparations of this plant include teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to make elderflower syrup, a popular remedy for coughs and respiratory issues.
